evals.prompt: Help me plan a new feature: real-time collaborative document editor. I have 2 weeks and a team of 3 (1 backend, 1 frontend, 1 devops). Users need to edit simultaneously and see changes instantly. We want to keep it simple. What should I plan for? What are the big decisions? What could go wrong?
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive planning guidance covering: problem clarification, key architectural decisions (CRDTs vs OT, WebSocket vs HTTP long-polling, centralized vs distributed), phases breakdown (1-2 weeks), risk assessment, and planning document structure

evals.prompt: I'm torn between two architecture approaches for my system: Option A (monolith with async jobs) vs Option B (microservices). Help me think through the trade-offs. We have 4 engineers, 6-week timeline, and want to iterate fast. Which should we choose?
evals.expected_output: Clear trade-off analysis with: pros/cons table for each option, implications for each (team structure, deployment, scaling, debugging), which best fits the constraints, and decision guidance based on the user's specific situation

evals.prompt: Help me structure a planning document for a new project. I have the product vision and architectural decisions figured out. What should I document? In what order? How detailed? What common mistakes should I avoid?
evals.expected_output: Planning document structure guide with: recommended sections (product/decisions/architecture/build/stretch), what each section should contain, example table of contents, depth guidance, and checklist for common pitfalls
